2604.18203.title: Multiplication in Multimodal LLMs: Computation with Text, Image, and Audio Inputs
2604.18203.paper_summary: Multimodal LLMs can accurately perceive numerical content across modalities yet fail to perform exact multi-digit multiplication when the identical underlying arithmetic problem is presented as numerals, number words, images, or in audio form. Because existing benchmarks often lack systematically paired instances across modalities, it remains difficult to compare genuine arithmetic limits within and across model families. We therefore introduce a controlled multimodal multiplication benchmark that factorially varies digit length, digit sparsity, representation (e.g., numerals vs. number words), and modality (text, rendered images, audio), with paired instances from a reproducible generator. We also define arithmetic load, C, as the product of the total and non-zero digit count as a compact, mechanistically motivated proxy for operation count. Across evaluations, accuracy falls sharply as C grows, often nearing zero by C > 100. Indeed, C remains predictive of performance across modalities and models, with R-squared often > 0.5, nearing the value from more complex measures of arithmetic load that count the number of intermediate arithmetic steps. A separate perception-versus-computation decomposition shows that multimodal degradation is primarily computational rather than perceptual: on matched-perception checks, models are near-perfect (> 99%) across modalities, even when multiplication accuracy drops. Beyond measuring when models fail, we ask which procedures they are predisposed to follow. We introduce a forced-completion loss probe that scores heuristic-specific reasoning prefixes--including columnar multiplication, distributive decomposition, and rounding/compensation. Here, decomposition is favored in both text and vision modalities; heuristic-specific LoRA adapters produce near-orthogonal updates yet degrade accuracy, indicating the base model maintains a well-tuned internal router.
2604.18203.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型能夠準確地感知跨模態的數字內容，但在相同的基礎算術問題以數字、數字詞、圖像或音頻形式呈現時，卻無法進行精確的多位數乘法。由於現有的基準測試通常缺乏系統性配對的跨模態實例，因此在模型家族內部及之間比較真正的算術極限仍然困難。因此，我們引入了一個受控的多模態乘法基準，該基準在數字長度、數字稀疏性、表示（例如，數字與數字詞）和模態（文本、渲染圖像、音頻）上進行階乘變化，並從可重現的生成器中獲取配對實例。我們還將算術負載 C 定義為總數字和非零數字計數的乘積，作為操作計數的簡潔、機械性驅動的代理。在評估中，隨著 C 的增長，準確性急劇下降，當 C > 100 時，準確性往往接近於零。事實上，C 在跨模態和模型的性能預測中仍然具有預測性，R平方值通常大於 0.5，接近於更複雜的算術負載測量，其中計算中間算術步驟的數量。單獨的感知與計算分解顯示，多模態退化主要是計算性的而非感知性的：在匹配感知檢查中，模型在各模態中幾乎完美（> 99%），即使乘法準確性下降。除了測量模型失敗的情況外，我們還詢問它們傾向於遵循哪些程序。我們引入了一個強制完成損失探針，該探針對啟發式特定推理前綴進行打分，包括列乘法、分配分解和四捨五入/補償。在這裡，分解在文本和視覺模態中都受到青睞；啟發式特定的 LoRA 適配器產生近乎正交的更新，但卻降低了準確性，這表明基礎模型保持著良好調整的內部路由器。
2604.18203.author: Samuel G. Balter et.al.
2604.18203.authors: Samuel G. Balter, Ethan Jerzak, Connor T. Jerzak
